verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a canonical secreted growth factor and member of the PDGF/VEGF family. It is produced with a signal peptide, secreted as a disulfide-linked homodimer, and functions as a soluble heparin-binding ligand that binds extracellular matrix and transient cell-surface receptors (VEGFR1/2/3, neuropilin). It has no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or stable post-translational anchoring to surface partners. Therapeutic anti-VEGF antibodies target the circulating secreted form, not a surface-displayed protein. The protein body does not stably reach the outer leaflet by its own mechanism.
